title: Italy’s Cinque Terre coastline could be hit by 13-metre waves by 2150 as sea levels rise
content: Beaches, ports and, during extreme events, even the railway line that runs through the Cinque Terre National Park are at risk.

title: Deep learning completes US flood hazard maps revealing millions exposed to previously unrecognized risk
content: This paper presents a deep-learning framework that learns from existing records to produce a spatially complete 30-m flood hazard map for the contiguous US, filling unmapped regions and updating legacy extents.

title: Seismic capacity survey and earthquake risk assessment of self-built buildings in rural areas of Shanghai Municipality
content: This study conducted field research and theoretical calculations to gain an overall understanding of the seismic performance of these buildings.
snippet: This study conducted field research and theoretical calculations to gain an overall understanding of the seismic performance of these buildings.

title: Rapid assessment of flood-induced agricultural losses to support infrastructure and policy decisions
content: This study develops an integrated, stochastic modeling framework to quantify three distinct agricultural flood-damage aspects—prevented planting, unharvested acreage, and yield loss—in Chariton County, Missouri.

title: Why only a few wildfires become extreme
content: Hot, dry conditions set the stage, but it takes a short-lived local window of opportunity to produce an extreme wildfire

title: Unifying climate and disaster risk management: Linking UNDRR and the UNFCCC through the Nairobi Work Programme to strengthen adaptation

title: How UNDRR is helping Syria lay the foundation for a resilient future
content: Syria became one of the earliest countries to implement UNDRR's DELTA Resilience system, which will help it better understand and reduce the disaster risks it faces as it recovers from years of war.
snippet: Syria became one of the earliest countries to implement UNDRR's DELTA Resilience system, which will help it better understand and reduce the disaster risks it faces as it recovers from years of war.

title: A tool can help protect communities from flooding and optimize reservoir management during rain-on-snow events
content: Beyond helping emergency managers better alert downstream communities to flooding events, tracking rapid runoff allows water managers to improve reservoir management.
snippet: Beyond helping emergency managers better alert downstream communities to flooding events, tracking rapid runoff allows water managers to improve reservoir management.

title: UCF coastal expert: Extreme coastal water level events are now 12 times more likely to occur
content: In 1900, coastal communities could expect certain extreme water level events to occur on average once in a century; in other words there was only a 1% chance to experience such an event in any given year.

title: Predicting hydrological drought at global scale: an analysis of the CEMS seasonal forecasts
content: This study evaluates the performance of forecasts in detecting hydrological drought in the period 1991–2022 using the ECMWF reanalysis ERA5 as a proxy for the observational reference.
snippet: This study evaluates the performance of forecasts in detecting hydrological drought in the period 1991–2022 using the ECMWF reanalysis ERA5 as a proxy for the observational reference.

title: Rethinking architectural education post-disaster: an analytical framework for curricular transformation after the 2023 Kahramanmaraş earthquakes
content: This study conducts a comparative analysis of the presence and evolution of disaster and earthquake-focused courses in Turkish public and foundation universities between 2022 and 2025.
snippet: This study conducts a comparative analysis of the presence and evolution of disaster and earthquake-focused courses in Turkish public and foundation universities between 2022 and 2025.

title: A novel approach to quantify cover-collapse sinkhole occurrences using multitemporal lidar
content: The researchers developed a novel method that detects elevation changes between two successive high-resolution lidar-derived digital elevation models and analyzes these changes to identify cover-collapse sinkholes that occurred over a defined time frame.

title: Building a Safer Society through Science and Technology: A Compendium of Korea’s Science and Technology-based Disaster Management

title: Heat waves increase wildfire risk – a new study explains how much, and it’s not a small number

title: Louisiana’s managed retreat — Model for coastal climate adaptation worldwide?
